---
title: "Revisions Over Time"
sidebar: scenarios
---

Field metadata changes. The proposal treats corrections and new interpretations as normal inventory operations.

## Typical Corrections

- A sample rate was entered incorrectly.
- A tap-test distance was later corrected.
- A fiber break shortened the active path for part of the deployment.
- A splice repair changed the path after a known date.

## Proposal Sketch

```python
resolved = inventory.networks[0].fiber_arrays[0].acquisitions[0]

corrected = resolved.revise(
    acquisition_sample_rate=250.0,
    comment="Corrected from field notes.",
)

inventory = inventory.replace(corrected)
```

The intended behavior is that loaded inventory objects have enough runtime identity for `replace` to update the right location without asking users to manually edit a nested manifest path.

## Data-Time Validity

Timed objects can use `start_time` and `end_time` to represent validity history. Patch resolution then chooses the inventory context appropriate to the patch time.
